title: Velvet Filter Boulevard
prompt: Create an original French touch track at 124 BPM in F minor, built on a chunky four-on-the-floor groove, crisp claps, bright hats, and a rubbery disco bass loop with tasteful sidechain pump. Use filtered funk guitar chops, warm electric piano stabs, analog string pads, talkbox-like synth texture, and original chopped vocal micro-samples that feel rhythmic rather than lyrical. Start with a low-pass filtered intro, move into section A with drums and bass, build by slowly opening the filter and adding claps, then hit a glossy chorus/drop with full chords and wide stereo sparkle. Add a breakdown with muted kick, vocal chops, and rising strings before a final lift with thicker bass and brighter hats. Mix it punchy, compressed, shiny, and club-ready with clean highs and controlled low end.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, uncleared samples, direct disco sample loops, harsh EDM supersaws, trap hi-hat rolls, distorted gabber kicks, muddy sidechain pumping
